finding: The cross-platform workflow built retained crash-stress observations but did not execute the existing byte-binding verifier before artifact upload. A malformed or mismatched observation could therefore be retained without an adjacent verifier result.
design_implication: Run verify-crash-stress-observation.mjs on each platform after observation construction and before artifact upload, bind the verification to GITHUB_SHA, retain verification-${RUNNER_OS}.json beside the source bytes, and execute both builder and verifier contract tests.

performed: Fetched and inspected the current workflow source.
Fetched and inspected verifier CLI arguments and fail-closed exit behavior.
Committed workflow wiring that invokes the verifier with observation, identity, summary, raw log, and exact GITHUB_SHA before upload.
not_performed: No hosted GitHub Actions run was observed during this run.
No Linux or macOS artifact was downloaded.
No cross-platform packet or classifier result was produced.

falsification_route: Dispatch or trigger the workflow on the committed descendant SHA.
Require both matrix jobs to upload verification files whose verified field is true and commit equals the workflow SHA.
Tamper with any retained identity, summary, log, or observation byte and confirm the verifier exits nonzero.
Reject the design if either platform uploads an artifact without a valid verification result.
epistemic_limit: This record proves only committed workflow wiring and source compatibility. It does not prove workflow execution, GitHub-hosted runner authenticity, filesystem semantics, successful crash recovery, or universal safety.
